title: Lightdash
description: Open-source BI platform that turns your dbt project into a self-serve analytics tool with explores, charts, and dashboards.
installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Lightdash web/api server (lightdash/lightdash, port 8080), which on boot also runs its own DB migrations against the bundled Postgres add-on. To be fully functional you MUST ALSO deploy a companion HEADLESS BROWSER deployment from image `ghcr.io/browserless/chromium:latest` (formerly browserless/chrome), exposing port 3000 on an in-cluster service named to match HEADLESS_BROWSER_HOST below; it renders charts/dashboards for scheduled deliveries, image downloads, and PDF exports. Lightdash also needs S3-COMPATIBLE OBJECT STORAGE for results/file caching and scheduled-delivery artifacts — point S3_ENDPOINT/S3_BUCKET/S3_REGION/S3_ACCESS_KEY/S3_SECRET_KEY at the RustFS S3 add-on or any S3 bucket (omitted from add-ons because it is not one of the supported operator datastores). ROTATE before exposing: LIGHTDASH_SECRET (`openssl rand -base64 32`; do NOT change it after data exists or saved warehouse credentials become undecryptable) and the Postgres add-on password (keep PGPASSWORD in sync). Set SITE_URL via the {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} token (already wired). The first user to register becomes the admin/owner. Optional outbound email needs the EMAIL_SMTP_* env vars (not provisioned).
